content.problemStatement:
Chef has met up with his old friend Jack recently. Jack told Chef that he found a recipe for an ancient dish, but unfortunately, it is locked in a safe and to open the safe, it is necessary to enter the solution to the following problem.

There is a sequence of non-negative integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ on the safe. Find the number of pairs (l,r)(l, r)(l,r) (1≤l≤r≤N1 \leq l \leq r \leq N1≤l≤r≤N) which satisfy the condition (Al∨Al+1∨…∨Ar)⊕(Al∧Al+1∧…∧Ar)≥max⁡(Al,Al+1,…,Ar) .(A_l \lor A_{l+1} \lor \ldots \lor A_r) \oplus (A_l \land A_{l+1} \land \ldots \land A_r) \geq \max(A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r) \,.(Al​∨Al+1​∨…∨Ar​)⊕(Al​∧Al+1​∧…∧Ar​)≥max(Al​,Al+1​,…,Ar​).

Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR, ∨\lor∨ denotes bitwise OR and ∧\land∧ denotes bitwise AND.

Jack and Chef cannot think of anything except the recipe, so you are asked to open the safe.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (10 points):** for each valid iii, AiA_iAi​ is a power of 222
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of pairs (l,r)(l,r)(l,r) that satisfy the given condition.
